qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Disabled user accounts retain Git HTTP and LFS access due to missing ProhibitLogin and IsActive checks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies missing account-status checks in Git HTTP/LFS authentication handlers (internal/route/repo/http.go, internal/route/lfs/route.go). The advisory describes argument injection in release tag deletion (internal/database/release.go) where rel.TagName is passed to git tag -d without -- separator. These are completely different vulnerable functions, root causes, and vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes argument injection (CWE-88) in Gogs release tag deletion where rel.TagName is passed to git tag -d without the -- end-of-options separator in internal/database/release.go. The sole qualifying finding addresses an authorization bypass in Git HTTP/LFS handlers due to missing ProhibitLogin/IsActive checks. It does not involve the release.go tag deletion code path, argument injection, or the -- separator issue.
evidence: Advisory root cause: process.ExecDir(..., "git", "tag", "-d", rel.TagName) in internal/database/release.go lacks -- separator before user-controlled rel.TagName.
prohibited-user-git-access-bypass root cause: Missing ProhibitLogin/IsActive checks in HTTPContexter and LFS authenticate middleware in internal/route/repo/http.go and internal/route/lfs/route.go.
